---
pagetitle: The Dashboards of Our Discontents
author: "Aman Bhargava"
description: "Against the terminal public-data dashboard"
image: "attachments/odp/slide61-2.png"
open-graph: true

format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, style.scss]
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    center: true
    auto-stretch: false
    embed-resources: true

execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
revealjs-plugins:
  - pointer
---

## {.center .textcenter}

::: r-fit-text
The dashboards of<br>our discontents
:::


::: footer
[Vizchitra, 2026]{style="opacity:0.5;"}
:::


##

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column .colmid width="38%"}
**Project Gemini<br>Mission Control Center, NASA**
<br><br>
**Somewhat unattainable.**
:::
::: {.column width="62%"}
![](attachments/odp/slide02-1.png){.bleed}
:::
::::


##

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column .colmid width="38%"}
**Dholera City<br>Control Room.**
<br><br>
**Somewhat attainable.**
:::
::: {.column width="62%"}
![](attachments/odp/slide03-1.png){.bleed}
:::
::::


## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide04-1.png" background-size="80%"}
## {background-image="attachments/odp/looking-in.png" background-size="80%"}

## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide07-1.png" background-size="contain"}

<div class="callout-box" style="position:absolute; top:33%; left:30%; font-size:1.8em;">We get KPIs!</div>


## {.center .textcenter}

::: r-fit-text
This is not an aesthetics problem,<br>but one of misunderstood needs
:::

<!-- Diagram Chasing -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide09-1.png" background-size="70%"}


## {.center .textcenter}

How did we get here?

::: {.r-fit-text .fragment}
Dashboards let you cut ribbons
:::

:::notes
Any problem can be solved by the creation of a new dashboard, which gives the appearance of activity without the danger of results.
:::


## {background-image="attachments/odp/ribbon.png" background-size="80%"}


##

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column .colmid width="28%" }
**Nobody's taking pictures of a dataset being released**
:::
::: {.column width="72%"}
![](attachments/odp/slide12-1.png){.bleed}
:::
::::


## {background-image="attachments/odp/trend_launches.png" background-size="90%"}




<!-- slide 14 — kicker + statement -->
## {.center .textcenter}

How did we get here?

::: r-fit-text
Dashboards are<br>*the* familiar pattern.
:::


<!-- slide 15 — full-bleed dashboard + overlay title -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/dashboard.png" background-size="contain" background-color="#1A202C"}

[Line go up: I happy 😄]{.overlay-title .fragment style="font-size:2.4em;  background-color: #252F3F"}
[Line go down: I sad 😔]{.overlay-title .fragment style="font-size:2.4em;  background-color: #252F3F"}


<!-- slide 16 — left-aligned text block -->
## {.center .textcenter}

::: {.bi-audience}
**BI Audience**

Known person,<br>known question,<br>and authority to act
:::


<!-- slide 17 — scattered words -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/who.png" background-size="80%"}

<!-- slide 18 — comparison: muted vs active -->
##

:::: {.columns .left }
::: {.column .bi-audience .colmid width="50%"}
[**BI Audience**]{.muted}

[Known person,<br>known question,<br>and authority to act]{.muted}
:::
::: {.column .bi-audience .colmid .right width="50%"}
**Public**

Strangers,<br>no known questions,<br>cannot take action
:::
::::


<!-- slide 19 — statement + follow-on -->
## {.center .textcenter}

::: r-fit-text
**Their only possible action<br>is more work with the data**
:::

A story, a paper, a campaign, a tool


<!-- slide 20 — centered statement -->
## {.center .textcenter}

::: r-fit-text
Dashboards answer the <mark>known unknowns</mark>,<br>but close questions that can be asked  <br> about <mark>unknown unknowns</mark>
:::

:::notes
When you publish only a dashboard for public data, you're imposing your worldview on everyone else. You're saying "these are the only questions worth asking" and "this is the only way to look at it." dashboards are for known unknowns, but raw logs/querying are for unknown unknowns.
:::

<!-- slide 21 — centered statement -->
## {.center .textcenter}

::: r-fit-text
What can generous<br>infrastructure look like?
:::

[Spoiler: There's a Magic Solution]{.rainbow}



<!-- slide 22 — centered image -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide22-1.png" background-size="contain"}


<!-- slide 23 — article + face overlay -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide23-1.png" background-size="contain"}

<img src="attachments/odp/slide23-2.png" class="stack-img" style="top:52%; left:54%; width:13%;">


<!-- slide 24 — image-bleed left / text right -->
##

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](attachments/odp/slide24-1.png){.bleed}
:::
::: {.column .colmid width="50%"}
**State censorship is recorded<br>in certificates near the toilet<br>in my nearest theater**
:::
::::


<!-- slide 25 — centered image -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide25-1.png" background-size="contain"}


<!-- slide 26 — centered image -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide26-1.png" background-size="contain"}


<!-- slide 27 — text left / image right -->
##

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column .colmid width="40%"}
**The Government had<br>other plans in store**
:::
::: {.column width="60%"}
![](attachments/odp/slide27-1.png){.bleed .contain}
:::
::::


<!-- slide 28 — full-bleed dark -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide28-1.png" background-size="contain" background-color="#0d1117"}


<!-- slide 29 — full-bleed -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide29-1.png" background-size="contain"}


<!-- slide 30 — kicker + image below -->
## {.textcenter}

[Horses? Maps? Shah Rukh Khan?]{style="font-size:1.3em; font-weight:800;"}

![](attachments/odp/slide30-1.png){width="78%"}


<!-- slide 31 — heading + URL list + chart -->
## {.textcenter}

[Permalinks!]{style="font-size:1.4em; font-weight:800;"}

Why would you *not* want your users to share things? Everything is linkable!

::: {.urls style="display:inline-block; text-align:left;"}
- https://cbfc.watch/film/sinners-2025
- https://cbfc.watch/browse/actors/fahadh-faasil
- https://cbfc.watch/browse/content/religious
- https://cbfc.watch/search?q=maps+language=English
:::

![](attachments/odp/slide31-1.png){width="55%"}


<!-- slide 32 — full-bleed -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide32-1.png" background-size="contain"}


<!-- slide 33 — full-bleed chat + caption strip -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide33-1.png" background-size="cover" background-color="#0d1117"}

<img src="attachments/odp/slide33-2.png" class="stack-img" style="bottom:6%; left:0; width:100%;">




<!-- slide 35 — image right / text bottom-left -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide35-1.png" background-size="contain" background-position="right"}

<div style="position:absolute; bottom:16%; left:3%; max-width:28%; font-size:1.4em; font-weight:800;">Confused about the data? I'll show you!</div>


<!-- slide 36 — article collage -->
## {background-color="#ffffff"}

<img src="attachments/odp/slide36-1.png" class="stack-img" style="top:6%; left:2%; width:56%;">
<img src="attachments/odp/slide36-2.png" class="stack-img" style="top:30%; right:1%; width:52%;">
<img src="attachments/odp/slide36-3.png" class="stack-img" style="bottom:5%; left:4%; width:54%;">



<!-- slide 40 — contribute page + chat overlay -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide40-1.png" background-size="contain"}

<img src="attachments/odp/slide40-2.png" class="stack-img" style="top:28%; left:12%; width:72%;">


## {.center .textcenter}

::: r-fit-text
There is more than one<br>way to public some data.
:::


<!-- slide 43 — centered statement -->
## {.center .textcenter}

::: r-fit-text
Who spends more time<br>cleaning up after meals?
:::


<!-- slide 44 — full-bleed -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide44-1.png" background-size="contain"}


<!-- slide 45 — full-bleed -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide45-1.png" background-size="contain"}


<!-- slide 46 — table image + caption below -->
## {.textcenter}

![](attachments/odp/slide46-1.png){width="64%"}

**Spread out across multiple<br>files and documents**


<!-- slide 47 — table image + caption below -->
## {.textcenter}

![](attachments/odp/slide47-1.png){width="64%"}

**Do the work and put it into<br>one usable file**


<!-- slide 49 — image + caption below -->
## {.textcenter}

![](attachments/odp/slide49-1.png){width="74%"}

**Run complex queries right in<br>your browser (no server!)**


<!-- slide 50 — heading + two images -->
## {.textcenter}

**Who spends more time<br>cleaning up after meals?**

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%" .textcenter}
![](attachments/odp/slide50-1.png){width="84%"}
:::
::: {.column width="50%" .textcenter}
![](attachments/odp/slide50-2.png){width="84%"}
:::
::::


<!-- slide 51 — heading + image below -->
## {.textcenter}

**Share your analysis<br>with anyone with a link**

![](attachments/odp/slide51-1.png){width="84%"}


<!-- slide 52 — full-bleed dark -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide52-1.png" background-size="contain" background-color="#000000"}


<!-- slide 53 — full-bleed -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide53-1.png" background-size="contain"}


<!-- slide 54 — centered statement -->
## {.center .textcenter}

::: r-fit-text
What can you do<br>for the public?
:::


<!-- slide 55 — two-tier statement -->
## {.center .textcenter}

A BI dashboard gets<br>**users, trust, distribution by default.**

<br>

With a public data interface,<br>**You get none.**


<!-- slide 56 — takeaway 1 (composed) -->
## {.center .textcenter background-color="#ffffff"}

::: {.urls style="position:absolute; top:4%; left:3%; text-align:left; font-size:0.6em;"}
dashboard.gov.in/?filters=location

cbfc.watch/film/animal-2023

app.powerbi.com/view?r=eyJrIjoiYjU3NDc3...
:::
<img src="attachments/odp/slide56-1.png" class="stack-img" style="top:5%; right:4%; width:24%;">
<img src="attachments/odp/slide56-2.png" class="stack-img" style="bottom:0; left:1%; width:16%;">
<img src="attachments/odp/slide56-3.png" class="stack-img" style="bottom:0; right:0; width:34%;">

::: r-fit-text
**Build so things can<br>travel outward**
:::

Maximize the surface area for shareability.


<!-- slide 57 — projects collage + centered callout -->
## You can't design for a user you don't know, so design trajectories instead

- I don't care about CBFC, but I care about Superman.
- Wait what else is censored??
- Give me all of it, I'll check myself.
- Keep me updated.

<!-- slide 58 — title + floppy image right -->
## {.textcenter}

[**Charts are only<br>an advertisement**]{style="font-size:1.6em;"}

Present your view, but know that people also want to do more work.

<img src="attachments/odp/slide58-1.png" class="stack-img" style="top:28%; right:2%; width:32%;">


<!-- slide 59 — two-tier statement -->
## {.center .textcenter}

::: r-fit-text
**Assume you<br>will lose interest**
:::

A public data interface outlives the individual interest of the person building it.


<!-- slide 60 — takeaway recap -->
## {.center}

::: {.takeaways}
Build so things can travel outward

Design for low friction, high delight

Charts cannot be the end point

Build for the day you lose interest
:::


<!-- slide 61 — mural + centered callout -->
## {background-image="attachments/odp/slide61-2.png" background-size="cover" .textcenter}

<div class="callout-box" style="font-size:1.5em; max-width:56%; text-align:center;">Let us build things that start something, not things where the reader stops</div>
